# Roomshare Search Page — UI/UX Improvement Plan

*Generated by 6-agent collaborative audit team, 2026-04-05*
*Approved unanimously by all 5 auditors (Round 2, 2026-04-05)*

## Executive Summary

The Roomshare search page has a solid architectural foundation — persistent map, cursor-based pagination, design tokens, and good semantic HTML structure. However, the audit uncovered **critical accessibility violations** (focus indicators stripped without replacements, `prefers-reduced-motion` not respected), **visual design inconsistencies** (mixed border-radius values, hardcoded colors bypassing the token system, inconsistent spacing), **mobile UX friction** (touch targets below 44px minimum, filter pill tap areas too small, bottom sheet gesture edge cases), and **interaction gaps** (missing loading/error micro-feedback, no hover states on several interactive elements). The plan below prioritizes accessibility and mobile fixes first, then visual consistency, then polish.

## Severity Definitions

- **P0 Critical**: Accessibility violations, broken functionality, unusable on a device class
- **P1 High**: Major UX friction, significant design inconsistencies, performance issues
- **P2 Medium**: Minor UX improvements, polish items, design consistency
- **P3 Low**: Nice-to-haves, micro-interactions, visual refinements

---

## P0 — Critical Issues

### P0-1: Filter pills missing focus-visible indicators
- **Found by**: accessibility-auditor, visual-design-auditor
- **Category**: A11y
- **Affected components**: `src/components/search/InlineFilterStrip.tsx:184-193` (FilterPill), `src/components/search/InlineFilterStrip.tsx:101-113` (Filters button), `src/components/search/InlineFilterStrip.tsx:121-131` (applied chip buttons)
- **Problem**: The FilterPill component and applied filter chip buttons have no `focus-visible:ring-*` styling. Keyboard users cannot see which filter is focused when tabbing through the strip.
- **Proposed fix**: Add `focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-primary/30 focus-visible:ring-offset-2` to all three button variants in InlineFilterStrip. The "Filters" button at line 101 and the chip removal buttons at line 121 need the same treatment.
- **Effort**: S
- **Dependencies**: None

### P0-2: prefers-reduced-motion not respected by Framer Motion animations
- **Found by**: accessibility-auditor, mobile-responsive-auditor
- **Category**: A11y (WCAG 2.3.3)
- **Affected components**: `src/components/search/MobileSearchOverlay.tsx:204-209` (slide-up), `src/components/search/MobileBottomSheet.tsx:363-376` (spring animations), `src/components/search/FloatingMapButton.tsx:41-44` (scale entrance), `src/components/map/MapMovedBanner.tsx:51-54` (slide-in), `src/components/search/MobileListingPreview.tsx` (scroll snap), `src/components/search/PullToRefresh.tsx:112-127` (pull indicator)
- **Problem**: Multiple Framer Motion `m.div` and `m.button` elements use spring/tween animations without checking `prefers-reduced-motion`. Users with motion sensitivity see full spring animations. The CSS card-entrance animation (globals.css:173-177) correctly respects reduced motion, but Framer Motion components do not.
- **Proposed fix**: Create a shared `useReducedMotion()` hook (Framer Motion provides `useReducedMotion`). Wrap all `transition` props to use `{ duration: 0 }` when reduced motion is preferred. Apply to MobileSearchOverlay (slide), MobileBottomSheet (spring snap), FloatingMapButton (scale), MapMovedBanner (slide), and PullToRefresh (pull indicator animation).
- **Effort**: M
- **Dependencies**: None

### P0-3: MobileBottomSheet shadow class has missing space — style not applied
- **Found by**: browser-inspector, visual-design-auditor
- **Category**: Visual / Mobile
- **Affected components**: `src/components/search/MobileBottomSheet.tsx:367`
- **Problem**: The shadow class is `shadow-[0_-4px_24px_rgba(0,0,0,0.12)][0_-4px_24px_rgba(0,0,0,0.4)]` — the two arbitrary shadow values are concatenated without a space. Tailwind silently drops the invalid class, so the sheet has no top shadow. On mobile, the sheet floats over the map with no visual separation.
- **Proposed fix**: Change to `shadow-[0_-4px_24px_rgba(0,0,0,0.12)]` (single shadow is sufficient). The dark mode variant was likely accidental since the project has no dark mode.
- **Effort**: S
- **Dependencies**: None

### P0-4: ListingCard article missing cursor-pointer
- **Found by**: ux-interaction-auditor, visual-design-auditor
- **Category**: UX
- **Affected components**: `src/components/listings/ListingCard.tsx:307-313`
- **Problem**: The `<article>` element wrapping each listing card does not have `cursor-pointer`. Users don't get the hand cursor when hovering over cards, making them look non-interactive despite being fully clickable links.
- **Proposed fix**: Add `cursor-pointer` to the article's className at line 308.
- **Effort**: S
- **Dependencies**: None

### P0-5: SortSelect mobile sheet missing aria-label on backdrop
- **Found by**: accessibility-auditor
- **Category**: A11y
- **Affected components**: `src/components/SortSelect.tsx:126-129`
- **Problem**: The mobile sort sheet's dialog wrapper at line 126 has `role="dialog"` and `aria-modal="true"` but the backdrop div at line 132 relies on `onClick` for dismissal without any keyboard equivalent. The sheet itself correctly handles Escape, but focus is not trapped — `FocusTrap` is used but the outer dialog wrapper does not have `tabIndex={-1}`.
- **Proposed fix**: The `FocusTrap` at line 138 handles focus trapping. Add `tabIndex={-1}` to the dialog wrapper div at line 126 to make it focusable for programmatic focus. No other change needed since Escape is already handled.
- **Effort**: S
- **Dependencies**: None

### P0-6: MobileSearchOverlay budget inputs strip focus with no replacement
- **Found by**: accessibility-auditor (A1 — CRITICAL)
- **Category**: A11y (WCAG 2.4.7)
- **Affected components**: `src/components/search/MobileSearchOverlay.tsx:278,289`
- **Problem**: Both min and max budget `<input>` elements have `focus:outline-none` with NO `focus-visible:ring-*` replacement. When keyboard users tab to these inputs, there is zero visible focus indicator. These sit inside a shared border container but neither the input nor the container provides focus feedback for the individual input.
- **Proposed fix**: Add `focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-primary/30` to both budget inputs at lines 278 and 289. Alternatively, add `focus-within:ring-2 focus-within:ring-primary/30` to the parent container AND visually distinguish which input is focused (e.g., underline the active input).
- **Effort**: S
- **Dependencies**: None

### P0-7: Bottom sheet missing "half" snap point — binary collapsed/expanded UX
- **Found by**: ux-interaction-auditor (M6), mobile-responsive-auditor (M1), browser-inspector (confirmed visually)
- **Category**: Mobile / UX
- **Affected components**: `src/lib/mobile-layout.ts` (defines only `SNAP_COLLAPSED=0.15`, `SNAP_EXPANDED=0.85`), `src/components/search/MobileBottomSheet.tsx:19` (`SNAP_POINTS = [SNAP_COLLAPSED, SNAP_EXPANDED]`)
- **Problem**: CLAUDE.md documents 3 snap points (collapsed ~15vh, half ~50vh, expanded ~85vh) but only 2 exist. The "half" position was removed. Code comments still reference "half" (line 35, line 305). Without the half snap point, users get an all-or-nothing experience — either a tiny 15vh sliver or a massive 85vh sheet. No comfortable "peek" mode to see results while keeping the map mostly visible. Browser-inspector confirmed: at 375px the sheet fills the entire viewport from search bar to bottom nav with zero map visibility.
- **Proposed fix**: Add `SNAP_HALF = 0.50` to `mobile-layout.ts`. Update `SNAP_POINTS` in MobileBottomSheet.tsx to `[SNAP_COLLAPSED, SNAP_HALF, SNAP_EXPANDED]`. Default to half on page load. Update snap index logic, body scroll lock (lock only at expanded), Escape key (collapse to half, not collapsed), and `aria-valuemax`/`aria-valuetext` to reflect 3 positions. Update stale comments at lines 35, 305.
- **Effort**: M
- **Dependencies**: None

### P0-8: Mobile search overlay closes before filter modal opens — double-transition flash
- **Found by**: ux-interaction-auditor (B1), browser-inspector (confirmed — overlay appeared not to open)
- **Category**: UX / Mobile
- **Affected components**: `src/components/search/MobileSearchOverlay.tsx:296-300`
- **Problem**: When user taps "Filters" inside the mobile search overlay, the code calls `onClose()` THEN `onOpenFilters?.()`. This fires the overlay slide-down animation, then the filter modal slide-up animation — two competing full-screen transitions in sequence. The user sees a flash of the underlying page between them. Browser-inspector observed: clicking "Expand search" on mobile appeared to not work, likely because the rapid close→open transition was invisible.
- **Proposed fix**: Either (a) keep the overlay mounted and layer the filter modal on top (FilterModal z-index is already 1200 vs overlay's 1200, so one needs to be higher), or (b) use a single transition: crossfade the overlay content into the filter modal content without closing/reopening. Option (a) is simpler — remove the `onClose()` call before `onOpenFilters?.()` and let the filter modal render above the overlay.
- **Effort**: M
- **Dependencies**: None

### P0-9: Desktop search bar shows "Anywhere" instead of active query
- **Found by**: browser-inspector (C3 — confirmed at 1440px, 1024px, 768px)
- **Category**: UX
- **Affected components**: `src/components/SearchHeaderWrapper.tsx` (compact search pill), `src/components/search/CompactSearchPill.tsx`
- **Problem**: At all desktop viewports, the search bar/pill shows "Anywhere" / "Any vibe" even when the page has active results for "New York". Only mobile (375px) correctly shows "New York" via the collapsed mobile search header. Desktop users cannot see what they searched for from the header — they must scroll down to the heading.
- **Proposed fix**: The CompactSearchPill and SearchHeaderWrapper need to read the current `q` search param and display it instead of "Anywhere". Check how the mobile `CollapsedMobileSearch` reads the query and apply the same pattern to the desktop compact pill.
- **Effort**: M
- **Dependencies**: None

---

## P1 — High Priority

### P1-1: Filter pills too small for reliable touch on mobile
- **Found by**: mobile-responsive-auditor, ux-interaction-auditor, browser-inspector
- **Category**: Mobile / UX
- **Affected components**: `src/components/search/InlineFilterStrip.tsx:184-193` (FilterPill)
- **Problem**: Filter pills use `px-3.5 py-2` which renders at approximately 36px tall — below the 44px minimum touch target. The applied filter chips at line 121 use `px-3 py-1.5`, making them even smaller (~30px tall). On mobile, users frequently miss-tap these.
- **Proposed fix**: Change FilterPill padding to `px-4 py-2.5` and add `min-h-[44px]` to ensure WCAG compliance. For applied chips, change `py-1.5` to `py-2` and add `min-h-[36px]` (chips can be slightly smaller since they have clear tap affordance via the X icon, but should be at least 36px). The "Clear all" text button at line 133 should get `min-h-[44px] px-3`.
- **Effort**: S
- **Dependencies**: None

### P1-2: SuggestedSearches pills have no hover state change
- **Found by**: visual-design-auditor, ux-interaction-auditor
- **Category**: Visual / UX
- **Affected components**: `src/components/search/SuggestedSearches.tsx:53,78`
- **Problem**: Both recent search pills (line 53) and popular area pills (line 78) use `bg-surface-container-high hover:bg-surface-container-high` — the hover state is identical to the default state. No visual feedback on hover.
- **Proposed fix**: Change `hover:bg-surface-container-high` to `hover:bg-outline-variant/30` on both lines to provide visible hover feedback.
- **Effort**: S
- **Dependencies**: None

### P1-3: Inconsistent border-radius across filter/search components
- **Found by**: visual-design-auditor
- **Category**: Visual
- **Affected components**: FilterPill (`rounded-full`), FilterModal fields (`rounded-xl`), MobileSearchOverlay inputs (`rounded-xl`), SaveSearchButton modal (`rounded-2xl`), CompactSearchPill (`rounded-full`), InlineFilterDrawer (via FilterModal), SortSelect mobile sheet (`rounded-t-2xl`), ListingCard (`rounded-2xl`), applied filter chips (`rounded-full`)
- **Problem**: The project defines `--radius-sm: 6px`, `--radius-md: 12px`, `--radius-lg: 16px`, `--radius-xl: 24px` in globals.css, but components use 4+ different Tailwind radius values without following the token scale. Interactive controls mix `rounded-full` (pills/chips) with `rounded-xl` (inputs) and `rounded-2xl` (cards/modals). While pills should be rounded-full by convention, the modal/card radius should be standardized.
- **Proposed fix**: Standardize: pills/chips = `rounded-full`, input fields = `rounded-xl` (24px, matching `--radius-xl`), cards = `rounded-2xl` (matching existing ListingCard), modal containers = `rounded-2xl`. No change needed for most components — this is already close. The main fix is ensuring SaveSearchButton modal input at line 214 uses `rounded-xl` consistently (currently `rounded-lg`).
- **Effort**: S
- **Dependencies**: None

### P1-4: "Show more places" button uses gradient that doesn't match design system
- **Found by**: visual-design-auditor, browser-inspector
- **Category**: Visual
- **Affected components**: `src/components/search/SearchResultsClient.tsx:541`
- **Problem**: The "Show more places" button uses `bg-gradient-to-br from-primary to-primary-container` which is the only gradient button in the search page. All other CTAs use flat solid colors. The gradient also creates a subtle contrast issue since `primary-container` (#b9583c) on `on-primary` (#ffffff) gives approximately 3.8:1 — below the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.
- **Proposed fix**: Replace `bg-gradient-to-br from-primary to-primary-container hover:from-primary hover:to-primary` with `bg-primary hover:bg-primary/90` to match the design system's flat button style (consistent with MobileSearchOverlay's search button at line 316). Remove `shadow-ambient-sm` as well — the primary color provides enough emphasis.
- **Effort**: S
- **Dependencies**: None

### P1-5: aria-live region on search results heading duplicates announcements
- **Found by**: accessibility-auditor
- **Category**: A11y
- **Affected components**: `src/app/search/page.tsx:391` (h1 with aria-live), `src/components/search/SearchResultsClient.tsx:362-374` (sr-only status), `src/components/search/SearchResultsLoadingWrapper.tsx:76-78` (sr-only status)
- **Problem**: The result count is announced by three separate `aria-live` regions: the h1 heading in page.tsx, the sr-only div in SearchResultsClient.tsx, and the sr-only span in SearchResultsLoadingWrapper.tsx. Screen readers may announce the count up to 3 times on each search, which is noisy and confusing.
- **Proposed fix**: Remove `aria-live="polite"` from the h1 at page.tsx:391 — the heading should be navigable but not auto-announced. Keep the sr-only status in SearchResultsClient.tsx:362-374 as the primary announcement. The SearchResultsLoadingWrapper.tsx announcement at line 76 should be kept but should only fire on filter transitions (it already does via the isPending guard). This reduces announcements to 1 on initial load, 1 on filter change.
- **Effort**: S
- **Dependencies**: None

### P1-6: SaveSearchButton modal uses bg-on-surface for primary action
- **Found by**: visual-design-auditor, ux-interaction-auditor
- **Category**: Visual / UX
- **Affected components**: `src/components/SaveSearchButton.tsx:325`
- **Problem**: The "Save Search" primary action button uses `bg-on-surface text-white` (near-black background) instead of `bg-primary text-on-primary` (terracotta). This makes the "Cancel" and "Save" buttons look reversed — the destructive-looking dark button is actually the primary positive action. The alert frequency buttons at line 278 have the same issue (`bg-on-surface text-white`).
- **Proposed fix**: Change `bg-on-surface text-white` to `bg-primary text-on-primary` at line 325. For the frequency buttons at line 278, change to `bg-primary text-on-primary`.
- **Effort**: S
- **Dependencies**: None

### P1-7: FloatingMapButton uses bg-zinc-900 — not from design token system
- **Found by**: visual-design-auditor
- **Category**: Visual
- **Affected components**: `src/components/search/FloatingMapButton.tsx:45`
- **Problem**: The floating map/list toggle uses `bg-zinc-900` and `shadow-zinc-900/30` which are raw Tailwind colors not from the Roomshare design token system. Every other component uses semantic colors (`bg-on-surface`, `bg-primary`, `bg-surface-container-lowest`).
- **Proposed fix**: Replace `bg-zinc-900` with `bg-on-surface` and `shadow-zinc-900/30` with `shadow-on-surface/30`. The visual difference is negligible (#1b1c19 vs #18181b) but maintains design system consistency.
- **Effort**: S
- **Dependencies**: None

### P1-8: CompactSearchPill divider uses wrong token
- **Found by**: visual-design-auditor
- **Category**: Visual
- **Affected components**: `src/components/search/CompactSearchPill.tsx:64`
- **Problem**: The segment dividers between location/price/type use `bg-surface-container-high` which is too similar to the button background (`bg-surface-container-lowest`), making the dividers nearly invisible.
- **Proposed fix**: Change `bg-surface-container-high` to `bg-outline-variant/40` at line 64, matching the divider style used in InlineFilterStrip.tsx:93.
- **Effort**: S
- **Dependencies**: None

### P1-9: SortSelect desktop trigger strips focus ring
- **Found by**: accessibility-auditor (B5 — MAJOR)
- **Category**: A11y (WCAG 2.4.7)
- **Affected components**: `src/components/SortSelect.tsx:191`
- **Problem**: The desktop sort dropdown trigger has `focus:ring-0` which explicitly removes the Radix SelectTrigger's default focus ring. The globals.css fallback outline may partially cover this, but the explicit removal is a risky override that could leave keyboard users without a focus indicator.
- **Proposed fix**: Remove `focus:ring-0` and add `focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-primary/30` to match the project convention.
- **Effort**: S
- **Dependencies**: None

### P1-10: RecommendedFilters contrast failure + sub-minimum font size
- **Found by**: accessibility-auditor (D2 — MAJOR)
- **Category**: A11y (WCAG 1.4.3 + font size)
- **Affected components**: `src/components/search/RecommendedFilters.tsx:94,103`
- **Problem**: Double violation — (1) `text-primary/50` at line 94 gives ~2.5:1 contrast ratio (needs 4.5:1 minimum), and (2) `text-[10px]` at line 94 is below the WCAG 12px minimum font size. The "Try" label is nearly invisible.
- **Proposed fix**: Change `text-primary/50 text-[10px]` to `text-primary/80 text-xs` (12px, 80% opacity gives ~4.5:1 contrast). For the filter pill text at line 103, change `text-primary/65` to `text-primary/80`.
- **Effort**: S
- **Dependencies**: None

### P1-11: Recent searches strip all existing filters
- **Found by**: ux-interaction-auditor (B2)
- **Category**: UX
- **Affected components**: `src/components/search/MobileSearchOverlay.tsx:185-192`
- **Problem**: `handleRecentClick` creates a brand new `URLSearchParams` with ONLY `q` set. All existing filters (price, room type, amenities, move-in date, lease duration, etc.) are discarded. If a user has carefully configured filters and taps a recent search to change location, they lose everything.
- **Proposed fix**: Preserve existing filter params from the current URL and only update `q` and location coordinates. Use `new URLSearchParams(window.location.search)` as the base, then override `q`/`lat`/`lng`/bounds. Remove old `q` first to avoid duplication.
- **Effort**: S
- **Dependencies**: None

### P1-12: Mobile initial load scrolled to bottom of results
- **Found by**: browser-inspector (C2)
- **Category**: Mobile / UX
- **Affected components**: `src/components/search/MobileBottomSheet.tsx`, `src/components/search/SearchResultsClient.tsx`
- **Problem**: At 375px, the view starts scrolled to the BOTTOM of results, showing the last listings. The user should see the first results on initial page load. This may be caused by the bottom sheet's snap animation or scroll restoration behavior.
- **Proposed fix**: Investigate scroll restoration — add `scrollTo(0, 0)` on mount in SearchResultsClient, or ensure the bottom sheet content container resets `scrollTop = 0` when the sheet mounts or when `searchParamsString` changes (new search).
- **Effort**: S
- **Dependencies**: P0-7 (bottom sheet snap point changes may affect scroll behavior)

---

## P2 — Medium Priority

### P2-1: ListingCard image hover scale causes layout reflow risk
- **Found by**: visual-design-auditor, mobile-responsive-auditor
- **Category**: Visual / Performance
- **Affected components**: `src/components/listings/ListingCard.tsx:347`
- **Problem**: The image carousel wrapper uses `group-hover:scale-105` which scales the image by 5% on hover. While `overflow-hidden` on the parent clips it, the transform still triggers compositor layer promotion for every card on screen. With 20+ cards visible, this creates unnecessary GPU memory usage.
- **Proposed fix**: Change `group-hover:scale-105` to `group-hover:scale-[1.02]` to reduce the scale effect while keeping the visual polish. The `transition-transform duration-1000 ease-out` at line 347 can be reduced to `duration-500` for a snappier feel.
- **Effort**: S
- **Dependencies**: None

### P2-2: Empty state border uses --outline-variant/20 — too subtle
- **Found by**: browser-inspector, ux-interaction-auditor
- **Category**: Visual / UX
- **Affected components**: `src/components/search/SearchResultsClient.tsx:384`
- **Problem**: The zero-results empty state uses `border-2 border-dashed border-outline-variant/20` which at 20% opacity is nearly invisible on the warm canvas background. The dashed border pattern combined with low opacity makes the empty state look like a rendering glitch rather than an intentional UI state.
- **Proposed fix**: Change `border-outline-variant/20` to `border-outline-variant/40` for better visibility. Alternatively, remove the dashed border entirely and rely on the icon + text + background differentiation (`bg-surface-canvas/50`).
- **Effort**: S
- **Dependencies**: None

### P2-3: Duplicate result count display in SearchResultsClient
- **Found by**: ux-interaction-auditor, visual-design-auditor
- **Category**: UX
- **Affected components**: `src/app/search/page.tsx:386-395` (h1 count), `src/components/search/SearchResultsClient.tsx:424-429` (p text count), `src/components/search/SearchResultsClient.tsx:600-604` (contextual footer count)
- **Problem**: The result count is shown in three places: the page heading ("42 places in New York"), the inline paragraph above cards ("42 places in New York"), and the contextual footer below all cards ("42 places in New York"). The heading and inline paragraph are visible simultaneously and show identical text, which wastes vertical space and looks redundant.
- **Proposed fix**: Remove the inline paragraph at SearchResultsClient.tsx:424-429. Keep the h1 heading count (it includes the "Showing 1-20" badge) and the footer count (provides context at the bottom of the list). This saves ~40px of vertical space on mobile.
- **Effort**: S
- **Dependencies**: None

### P2-4: ImageCarousel navigation dots accessibility
- **Found by**: accessibility-auditor
- **Category**: A11y
- **Affected components**: `src/components/listings/ImageCarousel.tsx` (dots navigation)
- **Problem**: The navigation dots at the bottom of the carousel need proper aria labels. The dot buttons should indicate which slide they navigate to (e.g., "Go to image 3 of 5").
- **Proposed fix**: Add `aria-label={`Go to image ${index + 1} of ${images.length}`}` to each dot button. Add `aria-current={index === selectedIndex ? 'true' : undefined}` to indicate the active slide.
- **Effort**: S
- **Dependencies**: None

### P2-5: InlineFilterStrip scrollbar not hidden on all browsers
- **Found by**: browser-inspector, mobile-responsive-auditor
- **Category**: Visual / Mobile
- **Affected components**: `src/components/search/InlineFilterStrip.tsx:65`
- **Problem**: The filter strip uses `overflow-x-auto hide-scrollbar` which relies on a CSS utility class. The `hide-scrollbar` class should use both `-webkit-scrollbar` (Chrome/Safari) and `scrollbar-width: none` (Firefox) and `-ms-overflow-style: none` (Edge). If the utility is missing or incomplete, a horizontal scrollbar appears below the filter pills on some browsers.
- **Proposed fix**: Verify the `hide-scrollbar` utility exists in globals.css. If not, add: `.hide-scrollbar::-webkit-scrollbar { display: none; } .hide-scrollbar { scrollbar-width: none; -ms-overflow-style: none; }`.
- **Effort**: S
- **Dependencies**: None

### P2-6: MapMovedBanner list variant uses hardcoded amber colors
- **Found by**: visual-design-auditor
- **Category**: Visual
- **Affected components**: `src/components/map/MapMovedBanner.tsx:91-117`
- **Problem**: The list variant uses `bg-amber-50`, `border-amber-200`, `text-amber-800`, `text-amber-700`, `text-amber-500`, `focus-visible:ring-amber-500` — 6 hardcoded amber shades not from the design token system. The map variant uses `bg-on-surface/90` which is from the token system.
- **Proposed fix**: Replace with semantic tokens: `bg-warning/10 border-warning/30 text-warning` for the container, and `text-on-surface` for the main text. Use `focus-visible:ring-primary/30` for focus rings (consistent with all other components).
- **Effort**: S
- **Dependencies**: None

### P2-7: DrawerZeroState uses hardcoded amber colors
- **Found by**: visual-design-auditor
- **Category**: Visual
- **Affected components**: `src/components/search/DrawerZeroState.tsx:24-38`
- **Problem**: Same issue as P2-6 — uses `border-amber-200`, `bg-amber-50`, `text-amber-600`, `text-amber-800`, `border-amber-300`, `hover:bg-amber-100` — 6 hardcoded amber colors. Should use the `--color-warning` token.
- **Proposed fix**: Replace with: container uses `border-warning/30 bg-warning/5`, icon uses `text-warning`, text uses `text-on-surface`, chip buttons use `border-warning/30 hover:bg-warning/10`.
- **Effort**: S
- **Dependencies**: P2-6 should be done first to establish the warning color pattern

### P2-8: SlotBadge uses hardcoded semantic colors
- **Found by**: visual-design-auditor
- **Category**: Visual
- **Affected components**: `src/components/listings/SlotBadge.tsx:15-18`
- **Problem**: Uses `text-green-700`, `text-blue-700`, `text-red-700` instead of design token colors `text-success`, `text-info`, `text-destructive`.
- **Proposed fix**: Change `overlayText` mapping: `success: "text-success"`, `info: "text-info"`, `destructive: "text-destructive"`. The project defines `--color-success: #2d7a3a`, `--color-info: #1e6fa0`, `--color-destructive: #c4321c` which are close to the hardcoded values.
- **Effort**: S
- **Dependencies**: None

### P2-9: Missing loading skeleton for FilterModal price histogram
- **Found by**: ux-interaction-auditor
- **Category**: UX
- **Affected components**: `src/components/search/FilterModal.tsx` (PriceRangeFilter section)
- **Problem**: When the filter modal opens, the price histogram data is fetched asynchronously from the facets API. During loading, the histogram area shows nothing — a blank space above the slider. This causes a layout shift when the histogram appears.
- **Proposed fix**: Add a skeleton placeholder (80px height, matching `HISTOGRAM_HEIGHT` in PriceRangeFilter.tsx:18) that shows while `priceHistogram` is null/loading. Use the same `animate-shimmer` pattern from loading.tsx.
- **Effort**: S
- **Dependencies**: None

### P2-10: MobileSearchOverlay close button touch target
- **Found by**: mobile-responsive-auditor
- **Category**: Mobile
- **Affected components**: `src/components/search/MobileSearchOverlay.tsx:218-222`
- **Problem**: The back arrow button correctly has `min-w-[44px] min-h-[44px]`, but the recent search "remove" button at line 359 uses `min-w-[36px] min-h-[36px]` which is below the 44px minimum. Since this is inside a list where mis-taps would delete a recent search, the consequence of a miss is destructive.
- **Proposed fix**: Change `min-w-[36px] min-h-[36px]` to `min-w-[44px] min-h-[44px]` at line 362.
- **Effort**: S
- **Dependencies**: None

### P2-11: PriceRangeFilter slider thumbs use focus: not focus-visible:
- **Found by**: accessibility-auditor (A3)
- **Category**: A11y
- **Affected components**: `src/components/search/PriceRangeFilter.tsx:97,101`
- **Problem**: Both Radix slider thumbs have `focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-primary/30` — using `focus:` instead of `focus-visible:`. This means the focus ring appears on mouse click too, which is inconsistent with all other components using `focus-visible:`.
- **Proposed fix**: Change `focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-primary/30` to `focus-visible:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-primary/30` on both thumb elements.
- **Effort**: S
- **Dependencies**: None

### P2-12: Skeleton grid gap mismatch causes layout shift on load
- **Found by**: visual-design-auditor (I4)
- **Category**: Visual / Performance (CLS)
- **Affected components**: `src/components/search/SearchResultsClient.tsx:461` (`gap-5 sm:gap-x-6 sm:gap-y-9`), `src/components/listings/ListingCardSkeleton.tsx:37` (`gap-4 sm:gap-x-6 sm:gap-y-8`), `src/app/search/loading.tsx:47` (`gap-4 sm:gap-x-6 sm:gap-y-8`)
- **Problem**: The actual results grid uses `gap-5` / `gap-y-9` but the skeleton uses `gap-4` / `gap-y-8`. When real content replaces the skeleton, the different gap values cause a visible layout shift (CLS issue). The skeleton content jumps when real listings render.
- **Proposed fix**: Change skeleton grid classes in both `ListingCardSkeleton.tsx:37` and `loading.tsx:47` to match `gap-5 sm:gap-x-6 sm:gap-y-9`.
- **Effort**: S
- **Dependencies**: None

### P2-13: MobileCardLayout SSR hydration flash
- **Found by**: mobile-responsive-auditor (M4)
- **Category**: Mobile / Performance (CLS)
- **Affected components**: `src/components/search/MobileCardLayout.tsx:29`
- **Problem**: During SSR, `useMediaQuery` returns `undefined`, so `isDesktop !== false` evaluates to `true`, rendering the desktop 2-column grid. On mobile devices, this causes a brief flash of the desktop layout before hydration switches to single-column. This is a layout shift (CLS issue).
- **Proposed fix**: Default to mobile layout during SSR: change `const showDesktopLayout = isDesktop !== false` to `const showDesktopLayout = isDesktop === true`. This way, SSR renders the mobile layout (single column), which is correct for the majority of mobile users. Desktop users get a brief single-column flash instead, which is less jarring since they have faster devices.
- **Effort**: S
- **Dependencies**: None

### P2-14: MapMovedBanner blocks map zoom controls
- **Found by**: ux-interaction-auditor (F8)
- **Category**: UX
- **Affected components**: `src/components/map/MapMovedBanner.tsx:56-78`
- **Problem**: The map variant banner is positioned `absolute top-16` with `z-[50]`, sitting directly over the map's zoom controls area. Users who want to zoom while the banner is visible accidentally tap the banner instead.
- **Proposed fix**: Move the banner to `top-2` or adjust its horizontal position to not overlap zoom controls. Alternatively, position it at the bottom of the map area above the attribution.
- **Effort**: S
- **Dependencies**: None

### P2-INV: INVESTIGATE — Map completely blank at all desktop viewports
- **Found by**: browser-inspector (C1 — highest severity visual finding)
- **Category**: Investigation
- **Affected components**: `src/components/PersistentMapWrapper.tsx`, `src/components/Map.tsx`, map tile configuration
- **Problem**: At 1440px, 1024px, and 768px, the map area is completely blank cream-colored space — no tiles render, no markers appear. The "Hide map" button is present but disabled. ~48% of the desktop viewport is wasted. This was observed in both `/search?q=New+York` and `/search` (browse state). This is the single largest visual problem on the page.
- **Proposed fix**: This requires investigation — likely causes include: (a) missing/invalid Mapbox API key in the dev environment, (b) map component not mounting due to a conditional render guard, (c) tile URL misconfiguration, (d) the map lazy-load / cost-optimization logic preventing initialization. **This should be investigated as a separate workstream** since it may be an environment-specific issue rather than a code bug.
- **Effort**: L (investigation)
- **Dependencies**: None — can be investigated independently

---

## P3 — Polish

### P3-1: ListingCard "No Photos" placeholder could be more informative
- **Found by**: browser-inspector, visual-design-auditor
- **Category**: Visual
- **Affected components**: `src/components/listings/ListingCard.tsx:354-361`
- **Problem**: The placeholder shows a generic Home icon with "NO PHOTOS" in uppercase tracked text. This is functional but feels cold. The uppercase tracking gives it a "SOLD OUT" vibe rather than a warm, inviting tone.
- **Proposed fix**: Change to sentence case "No photos yet" and optionally add a subtle prompt like "Photos coming soon" in smaller text below. Change the icon to `ImageOff` from lucide for better semantics.
- **Effort**: S
- **Dependencies**: None

### P3-2: ExpandSearchSuggestions skeleton animation mismatch
- **Found by**: visual-design-auditor
- **Category**: Visual
- **Affected components**: `src/components/search/ExpandSearchSuggestions.tsx:162-170`
- **Problem**: The skeleton placeholders use `animate-shimmer bg-gradient-to-r from-surface-container-high via-surface-canvas to-surface-container-high bg-[length:200%_100%]`. The actual rendered suggestions use a different visual treatment (icon circle + text). The skeleton shape doesn't match the final content shape — skeleton shows a round circle and a line, which is correct, but the heights don't perfectly match.
- **Proposed fix**: Ensure skeleton circle is `w-9 h-9` and text line is `h-4 w-48`, matching the final rendered content. Current values are already close — verify and adjust if needed.
- **Effort**: S
- **Dependencies**: None

### P3-3: TotalPriceToggle switch is smaller than standard
- **Found by**: accessibility-auditor, mobile-responsive-auditor
- **Category**: A11y / Mobile
- **Affected components**: `src/components/search/TotalPriceToggle.tsx:37-49`
- **Problem**: The toggle switch is `h-5 w-9` (20x36px) with a `h-3.5 w-3.5` (14px) knob. This is noticeably smaller than the SaveSearchButton toggle at line 250 which uses `h-6 w-11` (24x44px) with a `h-4 w-4` (16px) knob. Two different toggle sizes in the same search page creates inconsistency, and the smaller one is harder to tap on mobile.
- **Proposed fix**: Standardize to `h-6 w-11` with `h-4 w-4` knob for both toggles, matching the SaveSearchButton pattern.
- **Effort**: S
- **Dependencies**: None

### P3-4: Search error page uses bg-primary/10 for error icon
- **Found by**: visual-design-auditor
- **Category**: Visual
- **Affected components**: `src/app/search/error.tsx:30`
- **Problem**: The error page's icon background uses `bg-primary/10` (terracotta tint) and `text-primary` for the AlertCircle icon. Using the brand color for an error state is semantically incorrect — error states should use `--color-destructive`.
- **Proposed fix**: Change `bg-primary/10` to `bg-destructive/10` and `text-primary` to `text-destructive` at line 31.
- **Effort**: S
- **Dependencies**: None

### P3-5: SplitStayCard border class has extra /60
- **Found by**: visual-design-auditor
- **Category**: Visual (bug)
- **Affected components**: `src/components/search/SplitStayCard.tsx:45`
- **Problem**: The class `border-outline-variant/20/60` has a double opacity modifier. Tailwind will parse this as `border-outline-variant/20` and ignore the `/60`. It's not causing a visual bug but indicates a copy-paste error.
- **Proposed fix**: Change to `border-outline-variant/20`.
- **Effort**: S
- **Dependencies**: None

### P3-6: Add prefers-reduced-motion to card-entrance CSS animation delay
- **Found by**: accessibility-auditor
- **Category**: A11y
- **Affected components**: `src/components/search/SearchResultsClient.tsx:486`
- **Problem**: Each card has `style={{ animationDelay: `${index * 50}ms` }}` for staggered entrance. While globals.css:173-177 correctly disables the animation under `prefers-reduced-motion`, the inline delay style still causes the browser to allocate animation timelines. Minor perf concern on low-end devices with many cards.
- **Proposed fix**: Add a condition: `style={isReducedMotion ? undefined : { animationDelay: `${index * 50}ms` }}` using a `useReducedMotion()` hook. This pairs with P0-2's hook creation.
- **Effort**: S
- **Dependencies**: P0-2 (shared useReducedMotion hook)

---

## Implementation Sequence

### Phase 1: Critical Accessibility + Mobile (P0) — 1 PR
1. P0-1: Add focus-visible to filter pills
2. P0-2: Create useReducedMotion hook + apply to all Framer Motion animations
3. P0-3: Fix MobileBottomSheet shadow class
4. P0-4: Add cursor-pointer to ListingCard
5. P0-5: Fix SortSelect dialog tabIndex
6. P0-6: Fix MobileSearchOverlay budget input focus indicators
7. P0-7: Restore bottom sheet "half" snap point (M effort — larger change)
8. P0-8: Fix mobile overlay→filter double-transition flash (M effort)
9. P0-9: Fix desktop search bar to show active query (M effort)

### Phase 2: High Priority UX + Visual (P1) — 1 PR
1. P1-1: Increase filter pill touch targets
2. P1-2: Fix SuggestedSearches hover state
3. P1-3: Standardize border-radius (minor)
4. P1-4: Fix "Show more" button gradient
5. P1-5: Fix triple aria-live announcements
6. P1-6: Fix SaveSearchButton button colors
7. P1-7: Fix FloatingMapButton token usage
8. P1-8: Fix CompactSearchPill divider
9. P1-9: Fix SortSelect desktop focus ring
10. P1-10: Fix RecommendedFilters contrast + font size
11. P1-11: Preserve filters on recent search click
12. P1-12: Fix mobile initial scroll position (depends on P0-7)

### Phase 3: Medium Priority Polish (P2) — 1 PR

2. P2-6: Fix MapMovedBanner amber colors
3. P2-7: Fix DrawerZeroState amber colors (depends on P2-6 pattern)
4. P2-1: Reduce ListingCard hover scale
5. P2-2: Fix empty state border visibility
6. P2-3: Remove duplicate result count
7. P2-4: ImageCarousel dot accessibility
8. P2-5: Verify hide-scrollbar utility
9. P2-9: Add FilterModal histogram skeleton
10. P2-10: Fix MobileSearchOverlay remove button touch target
11. P2-11: Fix PriceRangeFilter focus: → focus-visible:
12. P2-12: Fix skeleton grid gap mismatch
13. P2-13: Fix MobileCardLayout SSR hydration flash
14. P2-14: Fix MapMovedBanner blocking zoom controls

### Phase 4: Polish (P3) — 1 PR

2. P3-4: Fix error page icon color
3. P3-3: Standardize toggle switch sizes
4. P3-1: Improve "No Photos" placeholder
5. P3-6: Conditional animation delay (after P0-2)
6. P3-2: Verify skeleton dimensions

### Separate Investigation
- P2-INV: Map blank at all desktop viewports — investigate root cause independently

---

## Design Principles Established

1. **All interactive elements must have `focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-primary/30 focus-visible:ring-offset-2`** — no exceptions. Do not use `outline-none` without a replacement.
2. **All Framer Motion animations must check `prefers-reduced-motion`** — use `duration: 0` when reduced motion is preferred.
3. **All colors must come from the design token system** — no hardcoded `text-gray-*`, `bg-amber-*`, `text-green-*`, etc. Use semantic tokens: `text-success`, `text-warning`, `text-destructive`, `text-info`.
4. **Touch targets must be >= 44px on mobile** — use `min-h-[44px] min-w-[44px]` on all buttons and interactive elements visible on mobile viewports.
5. **Primary action buttons use `bg-primary text-on-primary`** — never `bg-on-surface text-white` for positive actions.
6. **One aria-live announcement per event** — avoid multiple aria-live regions that announce the same content change.
7. **Pill-shaped elements use `rounded-full`, inputs use `rounded-xl`, cards/modals use `rounded-2xl`** — three radius tiers, consistently applied.
8. **No gradient backgrounds on buttons** — use flat primary color with hover opacity change.

---

- browser-inspector: **APPROVED** — all 38 items verified against visual observations
